1.1: the real gold that shone in Jerusalem and the stones from which the sanctuary was built; or, according to others, this gold represents the princes of Israel (see verse
1.2: Our heritage ; That is to say, the promised land that you gave to our fathers, and which we possessed by hereditary right. Foreigners ; the Chaldeans and neighboring peoples.
1.10: The pangs of hunger ; The Arabs say the fire of hunger , The Greeks, a burning hunger .
1.15: He filled me up with absinthe. . Proverbs
1.3: ostriches . It is said that she abandons some of their eggs in the desert, see Job ,
1.13: Under the woods with which they were charged, or with which they were struck.
1.16: The crown , etc. At parties, weddings and feasts, people crowned themselves with flowers.
1.20: the Lord's anointed ; This is understood literally as referring to Zedekiah, king of God's people, but in a higher sense, it should be understood as referring to Jesus Christ, the true anointed one, the only Son of God, taken and delivered to death because of our sins.
1.22: A prayer in which Jeremiah implores God's help to put an end to so many evils.
1.8: Slaves , etc.; according to some, these were the Chaldeans and Egyptians, also descended from Ham, whose descendants had been condemned to be slaves to Shem (see Genesis ,
1.6: . Desert ; also applies in biblical language to plains and countryside. The meaning of this verse therefore appears to be: We risked being killed if we went to seek out fruits or wild herbs in the deserted countryside for food, because the land was full of enemies and plunderers.
1.11: My liver , etc.; hyperbole, to express great pain. cf. Job ,
1.12: The Chaldeans, after beheading the princes of Judah, hung them by their hands from poles.
1.14: The door of the city, where the assemblies of judges were held.
1.29: prostrate your face to the ground.
1.33: God punishes men, forced to do so by their sins. Ezekiel
1.39: Often in Scripture sin is used to represent the penalty, the punishment for sin.
1.46: Contrary to alphabetical order, the word Phe is put before Ain (see verse
1.51: The girls from my town ; that is to say, the virgins of Jerusalem (see Lamentations ,
1.21: Daughter of Edom ; This is the nation of the Idumeans. country of Hus ; Idumea.
1.4: e initially appears to reproduce the paintings of the
1.19: Those who pursued us; an allusion to the Chaldeans, who pursued King Zedekiah with incredible speed and rapidity as he fled from them. cf.
1.26: ); according to others, the Idumeans, the Moabites and the Ammonites, peoples formerly subject to the Jews; finally, according to others, they were the very slaves of the Chaldeans, because it was the custom in houses where there were a certain number of slaves, that one of them should command the others.

2.1: How the Lord, in his anger, has covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud! He has cast down from heaven to earth the splendor of Israel; he has not remembered the footstool of his feet in the day of his anger. Beth.
2.2: The Lord has mercilessly destroyed all the dwellings of Jacob; in his fury he has overthrown the strongholds of the daughter of Judah, he has thrown them to the ground; he has profaned the kingdom and its princes. GIMEL.
2.3: In the heat of his anger, he shattered all the strength of Israel; he drew back his right hand before the enemy; he kindled in Jacob a blazing fire that consumed on all sides. DALETH.
2.4: He drew his bow like an enemy, his right hand was raised like that of an assailant, and he slaughtered all that was pleasing to the eye; in the tent of the daughter of Zion, he poured out his wrath like fire. HEY.
2.5: The Lord was like an enemy; he destroyed Israel, he destroyed all its palaces, he demolished its citadels, he heaped pain upon pain upon the daughter of Zion. VAV.
2.6: He has violated its enclosure, like a garden, he has destroyed its sanctuary. The Lord has put an end to the solemnities and Sabbaths in Zion; in the heat of his anger, he has rejected king and priest with contempt. ZAÏN.
2.7: The Lord detested his altar, abhorred his sanctuary; he handed over the walls of his fortresses to the enemy; there was shouting in the house of the Lord, as on a day of celebration. HETH.
2.8: The Lord plotted to overthrow the walls of the daughter of Zion; he stretched out the measuring line; he did not withdraw his hand until he had destroyed them; he made the wall and the forewall mourn; they lie sadly together. TETH.
2.9: Its gates are sunk into the ground; he has broken down and shattered its bars. Its king and princes are among the nations; there is no longer any law, not even its prophets receive visions from the Lord. JOD.
2.10: They sit on the ground in silence, the elders of the daughter of Zion; they have thrown dust on their heads; they are clothed in sackcloth; they bow their heads to the ground, the virgins of Jerusalem. CAPH.
2.11: My eyes are consumed with tears, my heart is in turmoil, my liver pours out on the ground, because of the wound of the daughter of my people, when children and infants faint in the city squares. LAMED.
2.12: They say to their mothers, «Where is there bread and wine?» And they fall as though struck by a sword, in the city squares, and their souls die in their mothers’ arms. MEM.
2.13: What can I say to you? Who is like you, daughter of Jerusalem? To whom can I compare you, virgin daughter of Zion, to comfort you? For your wound is as deep as the sea: who can heal you? NUN.
2.14: Your prophets have seen for you vain and foolish visions; they have not revealed your iniquity to you, so as to avert exile from you, but they have given you visions of lies and deceit. SAMECH.
2.15: They clap their hands over you, all who pass by on the road, they hiss, they shake their heads at the daughter of Jerusalem, "Is this the city that was called perfect in beauty, the joy of all the earth?" PHÉ.
2.16: They open their mouths against you, all your enemies; they hiss, they gnash their teeth, they say: "We have swallowed it up. This is the day we were waiting for; we have arrived at it, we see it." AÏN.
2.17: The Lord has done what he planned, he has fulfilled his word, which he spoke from of old; he has destroyed without pity, he has made the enemy rejoice over you, he has exalted the horn of your oppressors. TZADÉ.
2.18: Their hearts cry out to the Lord. O wall of the daughter of Zion, let your tears flow like a torrent, day and night; give you no respite, let your eyes have no rest. QOPH.
2.19: Arise, cry out in the night, at the beginning of the watches; pour out your heart like water before the face of the Lord. Lift up your hands to him for the lives of your little ones, who faint from hunger at the corners of every street. RESCH.
2.20: «Look, Lord, and consider: whom have you ever treated like this? What, women eat the fruit of their wombs, the little children they cherish? SIN. What. They are slaughtered in the sanctuary of the Lord, the priest and the prophet.”.
2.21: «They lie on the ground in the streets, the child and the old man, my virgins and my young men have fallen by the sword, you slaughtered in the day of your anger, you sacrificed without pity. THAV.”.
2.22: «You summoned my terrors from every side, as on a day of celebration; on the day of the Lord’s anger, there was no one who escaped or fled: those whom I had cherished and exalted, my enemy has exterminated.»

3.1: I am the man who has seen affliction under the staff of his fury.
3.2: He led me and made me walk in darkness and not in light,
3.3: He turns his hand over and over against me alone all day long. BETH.
3.4: He has worn away my flesh and skin, he has broken my bones.
3.5: He built against me, he surrounded me with bitterness and boredom.
3.6: He has made me dwell in darkness, like those who have long been dead. GHIMEL.
3.7: He surrounded me with a wall so I couldn't get out, he made my chains heavy.
3.8: Even when I cry out and plead, he shuts off all access to my prayer.
3.9: He has walled my paths with hewn stones, he has disrupted my ways. DALETH.
3.10: He was like a bear lying in wait for me, like a lion in ambush,
3.11: He has turned my ways aside and torn me to pieces; he has left me desolate.,
3.12: He strung his bow and placed me as the target of his arrows. HEY.
3.13: He drove the threads of his quiver deep into my loins,
3.14: I am the laughingstock of all my people, their song all day long.,
3.15: He filled me with bitterness, he gave me absinthe to drink. VAV.
3.16: And he made my teeth grind gravel, he plunged me into the ashes,
3.17: And my soul is violently torn from safety; I have forgotten happiness.,
3.18: And I said, «My strength is lost, and so is my hope in the Lord.» ZAÏN.
3.19: Remember my affliction and my suffering, the wormwood and the bitterness.
3.20: My soul constantly remembers it and is dejected within me.
3.21: This is what I will remember in my heart and this is why I will hope: HETH.
3.22: It is a grace from the Lord that we are not destroyed, for his mercies are not exhausted.
3.23: They are renewed every morning; great is your faithfulness.
3.24: «The Lord is my portion,» says my soul, “therefore I will hope in him.” TETH.
3.25: The Lord is good to those who hope in him, to the soul who seeks him.
3.26: It is good to wait in silence for the Lord's deliverance.
3.27: It is good for a man to bear the yoke from his youth. JOD.
3.28: Let him sit apart, in silence, if God requires it of him.
3.29: Let him put his mouth in the dust: perhaps there is hope.
3.30: Let him turn his cheek to the one who strikes him, let him be filled with shame. CAPH.
3.31: For the Lord does not reject forever,
3.32: But if he causes suffering, he has compassion, according to his great mercy.,
3.33: For it is not willingly that he humiliates and afflicts the children of men. LAMED.
3.34: When all the captives of the country are trampled underfoot,
3.35: when a man's rights are violated, in the face of the Most High,
3.36: When someone is wronged in their cause, the Lord would not see it. MEM.
3.37: Who spoke and it was done, without the Lord having commanded it?
3.38: Do not both good and evil proceed from the mouth of the Most High?
3.39: Why should a man complain while he lives? Let each one complain of his sins. NUN.
3.40: Let us examine our ways and scrutinize them and return to the Lord.
3.41: Let us lift our hearts, with our hands, to God in heaven:
3.42: «We have sinned, we have rebelled, and you have not forgiven.» SAMECH.
3.43: «"You were consumed by your anger and you pursued us, you killed without sparing,
3.44: You have covered yourself with a cloud, so that the prayer would not pass away,
3.45: You have made us like rubbish and scum among the peoples.» PHEA.
3.46: They open their mouths against us, all our enemies.
3.47: Terror and the pit were our lot, as well as devastation and ruin.
3.48: My eyes melt into a stream of tears, because of the ruin of the daughter of my people. AÏN.
3.49: My eye weeps and does not stop, because there is no respite,
3.50: until he looks down and sees the Lord from heaven.
3.51: My eye aches, my soul, because of all the girls in my city. TSADÉ.
3.52: They hunted me down like a sparrow, those who hate me without cause.
3.53: They wanted to destroy my life in the pit and they threw a stone at me.
3.54: The waters were rising above my head, I said, "I am lost." QOPH.
3.55: I called upon your name, Lord, from the deep pit,
3.56: You heard my voice: "Do not close your ear to my sighs, to my cries."«
3.57: You drew near on the day I called upon you and you said, «Do not be afraid.» RESCH.
3.58: Lord, you took up my cause, you saved my life.
3.59: You have seen, Lord, the violence they are doing to me, grant me justice.
3.60: You saw all their resentment, all their plots against me. SIN.
3.61: You have heard their insults, Lord, all their plots against me,
3.62: the words of my adversaries and what they plot against me all day long.
3.63: When they sit down or stand up, look: I am the subject of their songs. THAV.
3.64: Lord, you will repay them what they deserve, according to the work of their hands.,
3.65: You will give them blindness of heart; your curse will be upon them.
3.66: You will pursue them in anger and you will exterminate them from under your heaven, Lord.

4.1: How did the gold tarnish, how did the pure gold become debased, how were the sacred stones scattered to every street corner? BETH.
4.2: The noblest sons of Zion, valued at the weight of fine gold, how could they be counted as earthen vessels, the work of a potter's hands? GHIMEL.
4.3: Even jackals offer their teats and suckle their young, but the daughter of my people has become cruel, like ostriches in the desert. DALETH.
4.4: The infant's tongue sticks to the roof of his mouth because of thirst; little children ask for bread, and no one gives it to them. HEY.
4.5: Those who feasted on delicacies are now starving in the streets, those who were worn in purple are now embracing dung. VAV.
4.6: And the iniquity of the daughter of my people was greater than the sin of Sodom, which was overthrown in an instant, without any hand being raised against it. ZAÏN.
4.7: Her princes surpassed snow in brilliance, milk in whiteness; their bodies were more crimson than coral, their faces like sapphires. HETH.
4.8: Their appearance is darker than black itself; they are no longer recognizable in the streets. Their skin is stuck to their bones, dry as wood. TETH.
4.9: Happier are the victims of the sword than the victims of hunger, who slowly waste away, wounded, for want of the produce of the fields. JOD.
4.10: Compassionate women cooked their children with their own hands; they served them as food, in the disaster of the daughter of my people. CAPH.
4.11: The Lord has exhausted his fury; he has poured out the heat of his anger and kindled a fire in Zion that has devoured its foundations. LAMED.
4.12: Neither the kings of the earth, nor all the inhabitants of the world, believed that the adversary, the enemy, would enter the gates of Jerusalem. MEM.
4.13: It was because of the sins of her prophets, the iniquities of her priests, who shed the blood of the righteous within her walls. NUN.
4.14: They wandered like blind people through the streets, covered in blood, so that their clothes could not be touched. SAMECH.
4.15: «Get away! He’s unclean!» they shouted at them. «Get away! Get away! Don’t touch him!» They wandered about, and it was said among the nations, «Let them not stay here.» PHEAS.
4.16: The Lord's angry face scattered them; he no longer looked upon them. The enemy had no respect for the priests, nor pity for the elders. AÏN.
4.17: And we, our eyes still burning with anticipation of vain help, from the top of our towers, we gazed towards a nation that cannot save. TSADÉ.
4.18: They spied on our every move, preventing us from walking in our own places. Our end is near, our days are fulfilled, yes, our end has come. QOPH.
4.19: Those who pursued us were swifter than the eagles of the sky; they chased us over the mountains, they set ambushes for us in the desert. RESCH.
4.20: The breath of our nostrils, the Lord's anointed, was taken from their pits, he of whom we said: "In his shadow we shall live among the nations." SIN.
4.21: Rejoice and be glad, daughter of Edom, who dwells in the land of Hus. The cup will pass to you also; you will become drunk and undress. TABH.
4.22: Your iniquity has ended, daughter of Zion; he will no longer send you into exile. He will punish your iniquity, daughter of Edom; he will expose your sins.

5.1: Remember, Lord, what has happened to us, look and see our disgrace.
5.2: Our heritage has passed to strangers, our homes to unknown people.
5.3: We are orphans, without a father, our mothers are like widows.
5.4: We pay money to drink our water, and wood only comes to us for a wage.
5.5: Our persecutors are pressing us from behind, we are exhausted, there is no more rest for us.
5.6: We reach out to Egypt and Assyria, to fill ourselves with bread.
5.7: Our fathers sinned, they are no more, and we bear their iniquities.
5.8: Slaves rule over us; no one will deliver us from their hands.
5.9: We earn our bread at the risk of our lives, before the sword of the desert.
5.10: Our skin is burning like an oven, due to the pangs of hunger.
5.11: They have dishonored the women in Zion, the virgins in the cities of Judah.
5.12: Chiefs were hanged by their hands, the faces of the old men were not respected.
5.13: Teenagers carried the haystack, children staggered, laden with wood.
5.14: The old men stopped going to the door, the young men stopped playing their lyres.
5.15: The joy in our hearts has ceased, our dances have turned into mourning.
5.16: The crown has fallen from our head, yes, woe to us, for we have sinned.
5.17: This is why our hearts are sick, why our eyes are dimmed:
5.18: It is because Mount Zion is desolate and jackals roam there.
5.19: You, Lord, sit enthroned forever; your throne endures from age to age.
5.20: Why would you forget us forever, abandon us for so many long days?
5.21: Bring us back to you, Lord, and we will return; renew our days as before.
5.22: For would you have completely rejected us, would you be exceedingly angry with us?
